column26:
PureCircle Innovations tracks several operational metrics to monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of their programs and services. Some of these metrics include:
1. Number of beneficiaries served: PureCircle Innovations tracks the number of individuals and communities that have benefited from their programs and services, including job placement, skills development, and entrepreneurial support.
2. Employment outcomes: The organization tracks the number of individuals who have been placed in employment through their programs, as well as the retention rates of these individuals in their jobs.
3. Skills development outcomes: PureCircle Innovations tracks the number of individuals who have completed their skills development programs, as well as their performance in the job market after completing the program.
4. Entrepreneurship outcomes: The organization tracks the number of new businesses that have been started through their entrepreneurial support programs, as well as the success rates of these businesses.
5. Community development outcomes: PureCircle Innovations tracks the impact of their community development programs on the communities they serve, including improvements in education outcomes, health outcomes, and overall well-being.
6. Financial metrics: The organization tracks their financial performance, including revenue, expenses, and fundraising efforts, to ensure that they are operating efficiently and sustainably.
Overall, PureCircle Innovations tracks these and other operational metrics to ensure that they are delivering high-quality programs and services that have a positive impact on the communities they serve, while also operating efficiently and sustainably as a non-profit organization.

column39:
PureCircle Innovations's efforts to improve the Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH) conditions of Early Childhood Development (ECD) centers in South Africa had the following impacts to name a few:
1. Improved WASH conditions: PureCircle Innovations's efforts to rebuild and refurbish ECD centers and place new toilet systems have contributed to improving the WASH conditions in these centers. This has resulted in a cleaner and healthier environment for the children attending these centers, reducing the risk of illnesses and diseases.
2. Improved hygiene practices: By providing sufficient detergents and soaps, PureCircle Innovations has helped the ECD centers to maintain high levels of hygiene. This has encouraged good hygiene practices among the children attending the centers, promoting their overall health and well-being.
3. Improved nutrition: By providing nutritional value to the food and donating food parcels, PureCircle Innovations has helped to address food insecurity and malnutrition in the communities they serve. This has contributed to improving the health and development of the children attending the ECD centers.
4. Improved skills and knowledge: Through additional training for the practitioners and principals, PureCircle Innovations has helped to build the capacity of the ECD centers, improving the quality of education and care provided to the children attending these centers.
Overall, PureCircle Innovations's efforts to improve the WASH conditions of ECD centers have had a significant positive impact on the health, education, and well-being of the children attending these centers, as well as their families and communities.
